title: Benchmark + document the single-host final-assembly bottleneck (memory/time at scale)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The single-host final assembly is the known scaling bottleneck (memory/time), and quantifying it honestly is what justifies later frontier-consolidation work. It depends on merge-008.
objective: Benchmark and document the single-host final-assembly bottleneck (memory and time) across increasing precision targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmarks the single-host final-assembly step (memory and wall-time) across increasing precision targets with a documented, reproducible methodology.
Identifies and quantifies the single-host bottleneck and records the largest precision feasible on the reference host.
Documents candidate mitigations (e.g., out-of-core / large-memory consolidation) without overstating results; CC-BY-4.0.
output: A document reporting the benchmarked single-host final-assembly bottleneck and candidate mitigations.
